description: Add receiver-side delivered tombstones and a payload retransmit flag so late shared-route repair traffic does not cause duplicate delivery. Cover this with a receiver unit regression and a runtime/shared-route smoke test where one receiver repairs while another already delivered.

title: Implement datagram multipart transfer in route transport
description: Implement the datagram multipart protocol defined in docs/datagram_multipart_transfer.md inside the route-transport layer. Cover frame encoding/decoding, sender retention and id reuse policy, receiver reassembly and timeout handling, NeedParts bitmap handling, NoLongerAvailable responses, shared-route retransmission behavior, and tests for confusion recovery and checksum validation.

title: Design logical-payload transfer over datagram routes
description: Define how the lower route-transport layer handles logical payloads that do not fit into a single datagram. Cover fragmentation/framing, reassembly, missing-piece requests, spill-to-disk buffering, retransmission expectations, and how this integrates with route-local Ack/Nack semantics without leaking multipart details into GroupBroadcast or ReliableDelivery. This task must first survey relevant existing protocols and framing schemes before proposing a custom design.

title: Migrate protobuf runtime/codegen from protobuf v3 to buffa
description: The current protobuf crate in use is end-of-life, so migrate now while the codebase is still pre-production. Key points from the assessment: the .proto schemas themselves look compatible, but the handwritten transport layer is tightly coupled to rust-protobuf v3 APIs (MessageField, EnumOrUnknown, set_* oneof helpers, parse_from_bytes/write_to_bytes, generated ::new()). The migration surface is concentrated in flotsync_messages plus a small discovery follow-up. The current build uses pure Rust protobuf_codegen::Codegen::pure(); buffa instead requires either buffa-build with protoc, buffa-build with .use_buf(), or external buf generate, so CI/dev tooling must be updated accordingly. First implementation spike should confirm generated module paths for package-less protos, preserve current public module names if needed, and start with a narrow config (no JSON, no views, no use_bytes_type) before evaluating follow-up optimizations.

title: Design single-recipient durable delivery and relay mailbox protocol
description: Define a top-level protocol abstraction for durable delivery to one specific recipient outside normal GroupBroadcast fan-out. This is needed for bootstrap flows such as new-group establishment before a shared group key exists. Specify the relay-assisted mailbox mechanism needed to store and forward recipient-addressed encrypted payloads, how sender/recipient identity and retrieval work, how this interacts with later GroupBroadcast/Replication flows, and what delivery and persistence guarantees are required.
close_reason: Reviewed and completed via commit ff24597 (single-recipient durable delivery and relay mailbox protocol spec).

title: Define GroupBroadcast domain types and internal contracts
description: Introduce the code-level domain model needed for GroupBroadcast without building the full runtime yet. Define the envelope, delivery class, message reference, per-target queue state/outcome types, relay persistence confirmation shape, optional delivery receipt shape, and internal runtime-facing contracts needed between replication, broadcast, discovery, and persistence layers.
close_reason: Reviewed and completed via commit 128556f (delivery domain contracts and module scaffolding).

title: Map GroupBroadcast semantics onto flotsync_io Kompact transports
description: Define how GroupBroadcast delivery maps onto the current flotsync_io Kompact APIs. Specify when UDP versus TCP should be used, how transport-level send outcomes relate to semantic delivery, how relay persistence confirmations fit in, and which transport/backpressure signals the broadcast runtime must interpret. This task depends on the queue/state-machine specification.

title: Specify GroupBroadcast queue state machine and ownership model
description: Define the concrete GroupBroadcast state machine for per-message/per-target fan-out. Specify active states, terminal outcomes, legal transitions, retry triggers, ownership boundaries between discovery, broadcast, replication, and persistence, and which facts are internal bookkeeping versus externally observable. This task should produce an implementation-oriented spec that later runtime work can follow.
close_reason: Reviewed and completed via commit 34438dc (GroupBroadcast route queue semantics spec).

title: Prepare Replication runtime boundary for GroupBroadcast integration
description: After the queue/state model, transport mapping, and domain types are settled, extend the replication runtime boundary so a future implementation can wire GroupBroadcast in cleanly. Specify or add the internal hooks for enqueueing messages, receiving reachability updates, handling relay durability outcomes, and bridging replication events into GroupBroadcast without prematurely exposing transport details in the public Replication API.
design:
Agreed implementation scope for codex/flotsync-75s-replication-slice:

1. Make load_replication_runtime(...) return a concrete runtime behind the existing public ReplicationApi boundary.
2. Extend ReplicationStore minimally so the runtime can obtain the local member identity and dataset schemas it needs for this slice; do not attempt the full storage abstraction from flotsync-73q here.
3. Introduce a runtime-owned ordered hosted-group model alongside SharedGroupMemberships. SharedGroupMemberships/TrieSet remain the fast membership-presence view for delivery, but canonical member order must not be inferred from Trie iteration because TrieMap/TrieSet are backed by AHashMap and do not provide a stable order.
4. Build an internal runtime host around the existing delivery components so replication can update group membership snapshots, submit outbound work, and consume inbound deliveries.
5. Implement create_group for the fixed-membership, online-only path using ReliableDelivery for bootstrap. Do not support create_group.initial_state in this slice.
6. Materialise per-(group_id, dataset_id) schema-aware InMemoryData state and map public RowMutation values into local schema operations before transport.
7. Add the smallest useful replication wire/messages layer for this slice: one bootstrap message carried by ReliableDelivery and one live update message carried by GroupBroadcast. Do not try to complete the full Replication protocol state machine in this branch.
8. Implement publish_changes as: local apply, outbound GroupBroadcast fan-out, inbound remote apply, then ReplicationEvent::DataChanged emission on the receiving runtime.
9. Add one full-stack loopback test with two runtimes proving create_group followed by publish_changes produces a remote DataChanged event through the public API.
10. Keep the branch deliberately narrow and honest: fixed membership, live peers, direct routes only.

Explicit deferrals for this branch:

11. No change_group_membership implementation and no migration/invitation-policy flow beyond the bootstrap needed for create_group.
12. No Summary/NeedRange/Snapshot/Ack catch-up flow, no offline repair, and no causality-blocked replay beyond what falls out trivially from the narrow live-update path.
13. No relay/mailbox integration, no restart recovery, and no signature-verification enforcement yet.
notes:
Stage 1 implemented on codex/flotsync-75s-replication-slice: added minimal ReplicationStore hooks (local member identity plus dataset schema lookup), introduced runtime-owned HostedGroup/HostedGroups state with canonical member order and member-index lookup, and added unit tests for canonical ordering, duplicate rejection, and delivery snapshot derivation. load_replication_runtime now validates the store boundary by loading the local member identity before returning the current placeholder error.
Correction to stage 1: dropped the separate HostedGroup/HostedGroups model. SharedGroupMemberships remains authoritative, and GroupMemberships now stores GroupMembers (TrieMap-backed membership with fixed canonical member indices as values). GroupBroadcast was adjusted to iterate GroupMembers directly, and the stage-1 tests now cover indexed membership behaviour on the authoritative snapshot.
Stage 2 and the first half of stage 3 are now implemented. load_replication_runtime returns a concrete runtime with a fully wired internal delivery host, one externally bound UDP socket, and a background inbound-delivery loop. Added a minimal protobuf-backed runtime bootstrap message (group id plus canonical member order) and implemented create_group for the narrow first slice: it rejects initial_state, validates fixed membership including the local member, installs the group locally, and sends recipient-addressed reliable bootstrap envelopes to the remote members. Inbound reliable bootstrap messages install the same group membership snapshot remotely and complete the reliable-delivery processed promise so the ack path can run. Added an end-to-end replication test that loads two runtimes, publishes direct peer routes, calls create_group via the public API, and verifies remote membership installation with preserved canonical member indices. publish_changes, dataset state, group-broadcasted updates, and listener DataChanged events remain the next step.
Tightened the first runtime slice after review: GroupMembers is now canonical-order-only via from_ordered_members; the runtime host no longer relies on a fixed sleep for direct route publication and instead waits for route visibility in both semantic owners; ReplicationConfig now carries the external UDP bind address and defaults it to 0.0.0.0:0 so the runtime is no longer loopback-only by default. Added follow-up flotsync-46r for the ReliableDelivery sender-side recipient-ack timeout gap.
Implemented the first publish/apply replication slice on top of the delivery layer. Added a minimal runtime UpdateBatch message carrying one shared UpdateId plus per-dataset schema operations, runtime-owned hosted dataset state, local publish_changes staging/encoding/commit, inbound GroupBroadcast decode/apply, and listener-facing DataChanged emission. Added an end-to-end test that boots two runtimes, creates a group, publishes one row mutation, and verifies remote event delivery and replicated state. Verification: cargo test -p flotsync_replication; cargo clippy -p flotsync_replication --all-targets.
Implemented the review follow-up for the first replication slice: GroupMembers now only builds from canonical ordered members; hosted groups now track a real VersionVector instead of a local counter; UpdateBatch carries ReadVV on the runtime wire; inbound live updates deduplicate by UpdateId, buffer until LocalVV >= ReadVV and the producer version is the next expected one, then drain buffered updates once dependencies are satisfied; inbound delivery faults now terminate the runtime and fail subsequent API calls instead of being swallowed. Added focused tests for causal buffering/duplicate suppression. Follow-up tracking for future durability semantics: flotsync-74h.
2026-04-13 review-driven follow-up worklist agreed for this branch:

A. Architecture
1. Move the bulk of replication runtime logic into a Kompact ReplicationRuntimeComponent.
2. Keep the public ReplicationApi surface as a thin ask or tell bridge into that component.
3. Remove the external delivery loop and related imperative scaffolding once the component owns inbound and outbound delivery handling.

B. Runtime host and local endpoint binding
4. Split DeliveryRuntimeHost construction into separate build, connect, start, and shutdown phases.
5. Introduce readable local type aliases for long delivery port types and route transport types.
6. Replace repeated ad hoc connection error formatting with shared helpers.
7. Replace the UDP activation policy magic string with a named constant or helper for OnFirstUse.
8. Add a Kompact config key for runtime host control timeouts and use it for start, bind, and shutdown waits.
9. Move the local UDP bind concern out of public ReplicationConfig.
10. Introduce a simple internal local endpoint binder stub that owns one configured UDP bind for this slice.
11. Remove RuntimeUdpObserver as part of the binder stub work.
12. Make DeliveryRuntimeHost::drop a last resort guard after an explicit shutdown path exists.
13. Remove production use of flotsync.io.bind-reuse-address in the runtime host.

C. Discovery and temporary test scaffolding
14. Move RuntimeDiscoverySource into test support or otherwise isolate it clearly as temporary.
15. Document publish_route_update as temporary until real discovery wiring exists.
16. Clean up publish_direct_peer_route readability with local aliases or helpers.

D. Error and config surface
17. Remove unnecessary handwritten external constructors around boxed error sources and use idiomatic Snafu conversions instead.
18. Add a GroupMembers singleton convenience constructor.
19. Add sensible Default impls for SenderConfig, ReceiverConfig, and UDPourConfig, then use UDPourConfig::default() in the runtime host.

E. Wire format and payload handling
20. Add documentation comments to replication.proto that explain message expectations and ordering significance.
21. Rename read_vv to read_versions in proto and Rust.
22. Confirm and document that BootstrapGroup.members ordering is semantically significant and preserved on the wire.
23. Separate runtime in-memory message to protobuf conversion from protobuf to bytes conversion.
24. Keep bytes conversion at the use site for now, with a comment plus follow-up task reference for the later encryption-aligned payload redesign.
25. Stop forcing full version-vector encoding on the runtime wire. Support the compact VersionVector encodings properly.
26. Replace stringly decode_version_vector errors with a real internal error type and treat unsupported runtime wire shapes as bugs rather than soft strings.

F. Readability and tests
27. Remove test-only imports from top-level production scope in host.rs.
28. Use assert_matches! where appropriate.
29. Add GroupMembers tests that use multi-segment member identifiers.
30. Split runtime.rs into a folder module with separate tests and further submodules where sensible.
31. Trim import noise after the split, including broader local or wildcard imports where that genuinely improves readability.

G. Existing agreed follow-ups to keep referenced
32. Keep flotsync-46r referenced for ReliableDelivery sender-side recipient-ack timeout retry.
33. Keep flotsync-4x8 referenced for relaxing transient inbound delivery failures.
34. Keep flotsync-36j referenced for the UDP observer backlog concern until that code is removed.
35. Keep flotsync-665 referenced for the proper long-term local endpoint bind manager beyond the temporary stub.
36. Keep flotsync-74h referenced for persisting accepted local updates before returning PublishReceipt once durability exists.

Execution order for the branch from here:
37. Component inversion first.
38. Host and binder refactor second.
39. Wire and payload cleanup third.
40. File split and test cleanup last.
41. Verify with cargo test -p flotsync_replication and cargo clippy -p flotsync_replication --all-targets before the next push.
2026-04-15 review-plan alignment:
- R1 Error-surface cleanup across runtime/errors.rs and ApiError usage: add Display for GroupId and RowId, stop using debug formatting in user-facing errors, remove gratuitous Box<RowId>, merge or rename ambiguous variants, and promote obvious runtime conditions out of io::Error wrappers.
- R2 Runtime API ergonomics in runtime/handle.rs: introduce ApiResult/ApiFuture aliases, prefer actor ask conveniences over manual promise boilerplate, simplify or remove resolve_runtime_future if snafu wrapping can express the same behaviour directly, and remove slice wording from public docs.
- R3 Component visibility/test hygiene in runtime/component.rs: make component fields private again, group test-only messages under one nested test command path, and keep test-only access behind cfg(test) helpers instead of broad pub(super) exposure.
- R4 Local state backend abstraction in runtime/in_memory.rs: rename Hosted* to Local*, define an internal storage/state trait for the in-memory backend, move repeated group logic onto the group state type, move mutation-scope helpers closer to RowMutation or the storage contract, and push reusable VersionVector helpers to the right abstraction layer.
- R5 Message/wire simplification in runtime/messages.rs: clarify the domain/proto/wire separation, reduce unnecessary parallel DTOs where practical, and flatten large nested constructor transforms into named local fields.
- R6 Component control-flow refactor in runtime/component.rs: extract helpers for publish preflight, inbound apply readiness, listener notification, and reliable bootstrap handling; replace Vec-based de-duplication with HashSet where appropriate; move repeated can-apply and decode-with-member-count logic onto local group state.
- R7 Host-topology/orchestration redesign in runtime/host.rs is explicitly deferred to flotsync-3ht. Keep current PR bounded; only leave tracking comments where the deferral matters.
- R8 Test-infrastructure consolidation in runtime/tests.rs: replace the listener capture Mutex<VecDeque<_>> with a queue/channel-based recorder, record all events and filter in assertions, centralise timeout/eventually helpers, and add reusable fixtures for ids, schemas, and datasets.
- R9 Module-shell discipline in runtime/mod.rs: keep mod.rs minimal, move per-file imports local, and reconsider whether handle should become a public module rather than a re-export surface.
- C1 macOS CI failure in flotsync_udpour runtime_tests::repair_path_emits_need_parts_and_retransmits_only_missing_part appears to be a timer-processing race in the test harness, not a reproduced local protocol bug.
- C2 Preferred fix: use advance_time_and_process_due_timers for timer-driven assertions in that test and similar sites, document raw advance_time as logical-clock-only, and audit similar manual-time tests for the same assumption.
- Execution order: O1 R1+R2, O2 R4+R5+R6, O3 bounded R7/R9 handling with flotsync-3ht tracking left in place, O4 R8 + C1/C2.

title: Support flotsync_discovery on Windows CI
description: Make the discovery crates build and test on native Windows CI. Investigate the Packet.lib/Npcap or WinPcap dependency path from pnet, decide whether to install the required SDK in CI or make discovery networking support Windows-optional, and then restore flotsync_discovery and flotsync_discovery_cli coverage in the Windows job.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI workflows
description: Add GitHub Actions workflows for the Rust workspace. Require Linux CI on pull requests with fmt, clippy, workspace tests, and discovery feature-matrix checks. Add scheduled/manual native test runs for macOS and Windows and compile-only portability checks for mobile targets.

title: Make egress reservations growable or hint-based for send_with-style APIs
description: The new TcpSessionRef::send_with API currently requires callers to provide a hard upper bound for the serialized payload size. Revisit the egress reservation/write_with API so callers can treat the size as an optional hint or allow automatic growth during serialization without manual retry bookkeeping. This should preserve backpressure and fairness semantics while making the high-level send helpers significantly simpler to use correctly.

title: Improve flotsync_io Kompact open ergonomics
description: Make TCP open/listen futures resolve only when usable, remove redundant establishment events from steady-state streams, remove UDP socket reservation from the public Kompact API, switch UDP open requests to create-and-open with auto-assigned SocketId and UdpOpenRequestId correlation, and use UdpLocalBind consistently across UDP bind/connect. Update docs, examples, and tests accordingly.

title: Switch flotsync_io runtime logging to Kompact logger
description: Refactor non-test runtime logging in flotsync_io driver and pool code to use KompactLogger instead of the global log facade, add explicit logger injection to IoDriver, and isolate example-app logging so it no longer interferes with library/runtime logs. Discovered while finishing flotsync_io_examples netcat logging.

title: Add integration test matrix for correctness and flow control
description: Test lease starvation, suspend/resume transitions, UDP multicast/broadcast behavior, and TCP lifecycle edge cases.

title: Implement TCP listener and accept path (listener-owned connections)
description: Support bind/listen/accept and assign accepted connection ownership to the listener bridge for v1.
close_reason: Completed: implemented TCP listener bind/listen/close support, paused accepted-connection adoption via AdoptAccepted/RejectAccepted, Kompact TcpListener/PendingTcpSession/TcpListenerRef model, and raw plus Kompact test coverage for accept, reject, and drop-to-reject flows.

title: Implement explicit send ack/nack and suspend/resume semantics
description: Implement end-to-end explicit backpressure signaling for write and read paths without bounded queues.

title: Implement Kompact IoDriver and IoBridge typed integration
description: Expose the API via Kompact-native typed ports/channels with shared driver state and lightweight per-client bridges.

title: Pilot migration: replace Tokio-based UDP service path in discovery
description: Integrate flotsync_io into flotsync_discovery for UDP announcements and remove Tokio requirement on that path.

title: RFC: flotsync_io API and backpressure state machines
description: Define Rust-idiomatic typed port API for UDP/TCP freeform I/O, including ack/nack/suspend semantics and drop-based lease lifecycle for ingress+egress buffers. Scope: single mio driver thread and listener-owned accepts.
close_reason: Completed: added concise API draft at docs/flotsync_io_api.md reflecting agreed v1 constraints and naming.

title: Create flotsync_io crate skeleton with mio-first architecture
description: Add workspace crate and module layout for API, mio driver, and Kompact integration adapter surface. No protocol implementation yet.
close_reason: Completed: added flotsync_io workspace crate skeleton with api/driver/kompact/errors modules, moved kompact to workspace dependencies, and verified with cargo check -p flotsync_io -p flotsync_discovery.

title: Implement UDP broadcast and multicast support
description: Add broadcast enablement and IPv4/IPv6 multicast join/leave/interface/ttl controls to UDP sockets.

title: Implement single-thread mio driver core
description: Implement poll loop, token registry, wakeup path, socket table management, and command/event control flow for one driver thread.
close_reason: Completed: implemented a dedicated mio driver thread with crossbeam control-plane channel, waker-based wakeup path, handle and token registries, async command dispatch plumbing, and unit coverage for startup/shutdown, wakeups, FIFO draining, and slot reuse.

title: Implement shared ingress and egress buffer pools
description: Add reusable buffer pool layer with drop-based lease return semantics for both read and write paths, plus suspension/resume hooks when capacity is exhausted or restored.
close_reason: Completed: added shared ingress and egress pool handles with validated driver-owned config, async FIFO egress reservations, try-based ingress buffers with async capacity waiters, a drop-notified IoLease abstraction for lease-backed payloads, and unit coverage for fairness, exhaustion, wakeups, and reuse.

title: Implement UDP core: bind/send/recv for connected and unconnected sockets
description: Support core UDP operations and event flow for raw bytes on the new driver.
close_reason: Completed: implemented real mio-backed UDP bind/connect/send/recv/close handling with driver-owned socket state, event emission for success and failure paths, ingress-capacity read suspend/resume, reusable scratch-buffer linearisation for lease-backed datagrams, and UDP integration tests for unconnected, connected, invalid-target, and suspend/resume behavior.

title: Implement TCP stream client path
description: Support connect, raw byte send/recv, and close variants for outbound TCP streams with driver-managed lifecycle events.
close_reason: Completed: implemented outbound TCP stream client support with nonblocking connect completion, partial-write send progression, ingress-pool-based read suspend/resume, explicit connect failure reporting, graceful close-after-flush, abort close, and loopback coverage for connect/send/recv, failure, backpressure, graceful close, and ingress exhaustion.

title: Add freeform network I/O support in Kompact
description: Implement foundational support for freeform network I/O in Kompact so components can perform arbitrary network reads/writes beyond current constrained messaging paths.
close_reason: Completed: foundational freeform network I/O support and discovery migration are in place; soak/perf work deferred

title: Specify PeerDiscovery+Tracking protocol and auth rules
description: Define wire payloads and state transitions for Hello/CheckIn/StillHere, including Unknown/Known/Reachable/Stale lifecycle, nonce/challenge handling, and signature verification against group-scoped identity keys.

title: Design migration policy API (automatic and user-mediated)
description: Design a migration policy surface in the replication API that supports both fully automatic policies (non-user-facing apps) and user-mediated/semi-manual policies. Cover MigrationInit handling, acceptance/rejection hooks, GroupClose policy modes, and safe defaults.

title: Specify strict Ack semantics (G7 non-regression promise)
description: Produce a strict specification for Ack behavior matching protocol invariant G7. Define acceptance criteria, sender obligations, receiver checks, and edge cases (replay, gaps, coarse catch-up acks) so later implementation can enforce non-regression without compatibility ambiguity.

title: Specify Update mapping: SchemaOperation -> Replication Update(ReadVV)
description: Narrowed scope for the manual replication slice: the SchemaOperation -> Update(ReadVV) mapping is already implemented in the runtime. Local publish derives update_id from the durable local member version, captures read_versions before local apply, persists ReplicationUpdateRecord, and sends the same values in UpdateBatch. Inbound delivery validates group membership, sender index, duplicate/conflicting update ids, and causal readiness. Remaining work for this task is to document that existing contract and tighten inbound validation by rejecting operation change_id mismatches and impossible/self-dependent read_versions before bad inbound updates can become durable pending state.

title: Create flotsync_replication crate and define boundaries
description: Introduce a dedicated flotsync_replication crate as the home for replication-group and protocol orchestration APIs above schema-level operations. Define crate responsibilities and boundaries vs flotsync_core (VV/identifiers) and flotsync_messages (wire codecs). Deliverable is an architecture note and crate boundary contract, no implementation required in this task.
close_reason: Completed: added flotsync_replication crate to workspace and introduced initial app-facing dyn-trait API contracts, event listener model, row provider abstraction, and runtime/store boundaries.

title: Refactor broadcast envelope durability and relay indexing
description: Unify BroadcastEnvelope/RelayStore into one GroupMessageEnvelope with cleartext message_ref and delivery_class, remove RelayFetch, and align flows with NeedRange as best-effort broadcast request.

title: Add discovery identity-auth details and simplify broadcast active states
description: Update communication protocol spec to include key-based identity verification for Hello/CheckIn/StillHere and simplify GroupBroadcast active state model by demoting terminal outcomes to bookkeeping.

title: Restructure protocol spec into 3 sub-protocols
description: Refactor communication protocol spec to separate PeerDiscovery+Tracking, GroupBroadcast, and Replication with distinct state models, message sets, and cross-protocol flows.

title: Revise protocol spec after review round
description: Apply review feedback to communication_protocol_spec.md: global causality with read VV, directional state model clarity, Update vs UpdateBatch split, Ack promise semantics, migration-focused 6.6, remove NeedSnapshot and reset/recovery framing, and reduce session-oriented requirements.

title: Draft communication protocol spec for sync/migration
description: Create an implementation-oriented high-level protocol spec document for FlotSync covering session model, summary/range/update/ack semantics, replay/recovery, and replication group migration (including schema change via migration).

title: Build schema storage abstraction for operations and snapshots
description: Define a storage interface that applications embedding flotsync can implement to persist and retrieve schema-level replication artifacts, including SchemaOperations and Snapshots. Include clear API contracts for write/read semantics and expected consistency guarantees.
notes:
Aligned plan before implementation:
101. Change the storage abstraction so writes do not infer semantics from the shape of InMemoryData alone.
102. Introduce an explicit row-wise storage argument type that carries per-row Insert, Update, or Delete actions.
103. Change storage-touching transaction methods to borrow &mut self and tie borrowed inputs to the returned boxed future lifetime, so backends do not need async Mutexes or argument cloning.
104. Reuse shared version-vector and UpdateBatch codec helpers rather than duplicating encode/decode logic in the backend.
105. Move canonical member-identity text parsing into flotsync_core and consume that from storage code.
106. Audit crate-local wait helper usage and replace the timeout-style cases with kompact::prelude::block_until, leaving block_on only for genuine one-shot completion points.

title: Specify authenticated delivery header fields in protobuf
description: Define and apply protobuf-level documentation or custom options for delivery fields that are included in authenticated public-header bytes/AAD/signature transcripts. The goal is to make the protocol boundary explicit in the schema instead of relying only on Rust wire helpers. Start with delivery envelope and ack headers, then apply the same convention consistently to future signed or sealed frames.
