title: Add shared-memory cross-binary socket reservation coordinator
description: Follow-up from flotsync-jzo. Design and implement a cross-test-binary socket reservation coordinator using the shared_memory crate. Use a target-directory flink rendezvous file for the shared mapping, a fixed repr(C) layout for coordinator state, and an interprocess mutex/lock abstraction for safe updates. The coordinator should track process slots, request slots, lease slots, progress epochs, heartbeats, stale process reaping, and recent diagnostics so concurrently running Rust test binaries can decide whether socket acquisition is making global progress or is truly stuck.
acceptance_criteria: A shared-memory coordinator is created/opened through a target-directory flink rendezvous path; state uses a fixed layout with version/magic checks; updates are protected by an interprocess synchronisation primitive; processes register heartbeats separately from progress; stale process slots are reaped; socket waiters can diagnose live holders versus environment exhaustion across test binaries; focused multi-process tests cover progress, stale-process cleanup, and stuck-holder diagnostics.

title: Replace test JWKS key storage with production key provisioning
description: The reliable bootstrap/security refactor still uses deterministic test-support JWKS material and encrypted local-private-key records for runtime tests. Add the production provisioning path for local member private keys, trusted member public keys, and store-secret key handling so test scaffolding is no longer standing in for real key management.

title: Deduplicate replication store transaction read APIs
description: ReplicationStoreReadTransaction and ReplicationStoreTransaction currently duplicate read method declarations, including the member-security storage reads added by flotsync-sec.3. After this security-storage slice settles, extract a shared read surface or otherwise collapse the duplicated API so read methods are declared once.

title: Move local database secret storage to OS keyring
description: Replace the MVP plaintext application-config local database secret with OS-backed secure storage using the keyring crate. Preserve the store/setup boundary: application setup obtains or creates the device-local database secret, passes only the database-secret key or handle through setup as needed, and replication runtime continues to read provisioned security state from ReplicationStore rather than receiving identity or group secrets from live configuration.
design:
S9-01. Goal

Move the device-local store-secret key out of plaintext application config and into an OS-backed local secret facility, while keeping the public replication API opaque to that storage mechanism.

S9-02. Ownership boundary

`flotsync_security` owns the storage implementation details: keyring dependency, service/account naming, record encoding, random store-secret key generation, generated store-secret key id, load/create semantics, backend errors, and test-support helpers for isolated test storage.

S9-03. Public API shape

`flotsync_replication` exposes only application-facing security bootstrap concepts. Callers provide an application identifier and a local store-secret profile. They do not construct or inspect keyring entries, service names, account names, record bytes, or backend-specific handles.

S9-04. Lookup identity

The lookup key is application-scoped and profile-scoped, not member-scoped. Use `Identifier` plus a caller-chosen local profile string/value. Do not include `MemberIdentity`, because one application/store should later be able to host multiple local identities sharing the same store secret.

S9-05. Security-crate API

Add an opaque local store-secret profile type and load/create helpers in `flotsync_security`, e.g. `LocalStoreSecretProfile`, `LoadedLocalStoreSecret`, `load_local_store_secret(...)`, and `load_or_create_local_store_secret(...)`. The loaded value carries a generated store-secret key id and the `StoreSecretKey`; implementation names can be refined during coding.

S9-06. Replication wrapper

Refactor `ReplicationSecuritySecrets` so normal callers obtain it through `load_local(...)` or `load_or_create_local(...)` wrappers that delegate to `flotsync_security`, convert the security-crate key id into `StoreSecretKeyId`, and wrap the key for runtime loading.

S9-07. Constructor policy

Remove or gate raw store-secret-key constructors from the normal public application path. Keep test-support construction available where tests need deterministic in-memory fixtures, but avoid making raw key bytes the documented runtime setup path.

S9-08. Error model

Add Snafu errors for local store-secret load/create failures. Public replication errors should remain actionable and storage-opaque; storage-specific causes can stay in source chains without exposing keyring as part of the public API contract.

S9-09. Replicated checklist config

Replace `store-secret-password` with a local store-secret profile config field. Keep `group-secret-password` as the shared static group secret input from the previous task. Checklist instances on the same machine can choose different profiles to avoid clashing when running multiple local instances.

S9-10. Docs and sample config

Update replicated-checklist docs and sample TOML to describe the local profile as an application-local selector for the device secret. Mention that the current implementation stores the secret in OS-backed local storage, but do not document backend internals as the public interface.

S9-11. Tests

Add security-crate tests for first-run create, reload, profile isolation, and error behaviour using an isolated sample/test secret store. Add replication/checklist tests for wrapper loading, config parsing, and multi-profile non-collision. Existing runtime security tests should move from raw secret construction to test helpers where practical.

S9-12. Validation

Run `cargo fmt --all`, focused tests for security/replication/checklist changes, workspace clippy with `-D warnings -W clippy::pedantic`, and `git diff --check`. Before final response, compare implementation against this bd design and call out anything intentionally deferred.
acceptance_criteria: local database secret material is stored and retrieved through OS secure storage using keyring; first-run setup can create or initialise the secret; existing plaintext-config development path is removed or clearly gated for tests; sensitive store columns remain encrypted through the same database-secret boundary; and replicated-checklist docs describe the OS-backed setup flow.
notes: Implementation plan aligned on 2026-05-24: make StoreSecretKeyId an opaque UUID across layers; use fixed-layout local keyring records; use readable validated application/profile keyring account names; select persistent OS keyring backends explicitly (macOS keychain, iOS protected data, Android native, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service); install test sample store through one-time process-global initialisation; keep raw ReplicationSecuritySecrets::new only for test-support borked-key fixtures. Working note: .agent_local_docs/tmp/flotsync-sec9-keyring-plan.md

title: Introduce typed HPKE context for security APIs
description: Follow-up from flotsync_security review. The raw HPKE helpers currently accept opaque info/aad byte slices, which makes callers responsible for building protocol-domain separation and authenticated context exactly the same way on seal and open. Replace or wrap those arguments with a typed HPKE context owned by flotsync_security. The context should canonicalise the envelope purpose, sender and recipient member identities, group id, delivery message id, and authenticated public metadata into HPKE info/aad internally, so public callers cannot accidentally diverge.

title: Load private security files through zeroised buffers
description: Follow-up to the flotsync_security MVP. When config integration starts loading private identity JWKS files from disk, ensure private file contents are held in zeroised buffers or equivalent secret containers from read through parse, with clear APIs that avoid accidental long-lived plaintext copies.

title: Add passphrase protection for private JWKS files
description: Follow-up to the flotsync_security MVP. Add support for protecting local private identity JWKS files with a user-provided passphrase or equivalent local secret handling before these files become the normal long-term configuration format. The design should preserve the typed flotsync_security API boundary and avoid introducing a second application config parser.
close_reason: Won't do: these files are temporary and we are not going to keep this setup long-term.

title: Design migration UX for secure group changes
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: design the user and application-facing migration experience for secure group changes. This should cover how users review member additions or removals, how public keys are displayed or verified, how new group ids and group keys are accepted, and how applications handle denial or delayed acceptance.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete migration UX and API design covering user-mediated trust decisions, key display or verification, accept/reject flows, new group id installation, and failure handling; implementation tasks can be derived from it.

title: Design session encryption for relay and TCP communication
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: design session-oriented encryption for relay or TCP communication where multi-shot HPKE or another session protocol may reduce overhead and provide cleaner stream semantics. This should account for message ordering, retries, reconnects, relay authentication, and how session keys interact with envelope-level group encryption.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete design for relay or TCP session encryption, including handshake, key lifecycle, ordering assumptions, reconnect behaviour, failure handling, and tests or follow-up implementation tasks.

title: Publish and verify member public keys through discovery
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: move beyond configured trusted public JWKS files by integrating public key advertisement and verification into discovery or a related trust mechanism. This should settle how discovery announcements carry key material or key references, how keys are authenticated, and how runtime trust decisions avoid accepting spoofed identities.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery or a defined adjacent trust path can advertise member public keys or references; recipients can authenticate them before use; configured JWKS trust remains available as a bootstrap or override path; and tests cover spoofed announcements and key mismatch rejection.

title: Protect local identity JWKS files with passphrases
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: add passphrase-protected local identity files or an equivalent local secret protection mechanism. The design should decide whether to use encrypted JWK/JWE, age-like wrapping, OS keychain integration, or another approach, and should cover CLI prompts, non-interactive configuration, and test support.
acceptance_criteria: Local private identity material can be stored encrypted at rest with a documented unlock path; replicated-checklist or future CLIs can load protected identities; and tests cover wrong passphrase, successful unlock, and unprotected test fixtures.

title: Design flotsync_security key rotation and revocation
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: design and implement rotation and revocation for member identity keys and group symmetric keys. This should define how compromised member keys trigger group migration, how old keys remain usable for historical verification when appropriate, how revoked keys are represented, and what store/runtime migrations are required.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete rotation and revocation design covering member signing keys, member encryption keys, group keys, stored group metadata, migration interaction, and compatibility with existing groups; implementation tasks or code changes follow that design.

title: Peek runtime message type before full decode
description: Investigate a lightweight runtime-envelope decode path that lets components route or drop by message type before decoding full payloads. Catch-up and summary components currently decode full RuntimeMessage values only to ignore most variants.

title: Rethink replication component public API boundary
description: Summary requests currently enter through ReplicationRuntimeComponent and are temporarily forwarded to SummaryRequestManager. Revisit whether public API asks should be routed directly to capability-specific Kompact components or through a dedicated stable facade.

title: Support multi-fragment linear string updates in one publish
description: Discovered while testing flotsync-0ns. A sparse field update from one linear_string value to another can fail with LinearStringDiff::IdsExhausted when the text diff requires more than one inserted fragment, because modify_row currently gives linear_diff only one major operation id. This is independent of read-token rebasing but affects real checklist edits with non-trivial rename/text changes. Design a way to allocate/address multiple inserted fragments within one replicated update without breaking UpdateId/read_versions semantics.
close_reason: Fixed multi-fragment linear string updates and added reserved-index regression coverage

title: Make ReadToken opaquely serialisable
description: Follow-up to flotsync-0ns. The first manual-slice ReadToken can be an opaque in-memory API value because replicated-checklist is in-memory, but later slices need applications to persist and restore tokens without inspecting replication group scoping. Design and implement opaque serialisation/deserialisation for ReadToken with versioning and validation against the runtime/store state.

title: Unify row provider end-of-stream semantics
description: Follow-up in the manual replication slice. SnapshotRowProvider should not diverge permanently from the listener RowProvider contract: decide whether row providers should use Option<Batch> or another explicit end-of-stream signal, then apply the same convention consistently across snapshot and event row providers.

title: Add ReplicationStore conformance tests
description: Create a reusable public-facing conformance test suite for ReplicationStore implementations. It should encode expected dataset row storage semantics, including tombstone round-trip, missing-to-tombstone acceptance after desync, active-to-tombstone transitions, idempotent tombstone writes, and rejection of tombstone-to-active resurrection. This should be reusable by SQLite and future store implementations rather than duplicated as implementation-local tests.

title: Support structural dataset identifiers
description: DatasetId is currently a flat SQL-like identifier, so the replicated checklist example uses checklist_items even though the logical identity would be structural ["checklist", "items"]. Add support for structural dataset identifiers if we want dataset ids to align with member-style multi-segment identities. This likely touches the public API, store encoding, wire representation, and migrations/compatibility.

title: Implement durable GroupBroadcast relay/work persistence
description:
Deferred follow-up outside the manual replication slice.

Current status: the manual slice sends live UpdateBatch traffic through GroupBroadcast as best-effort direct fan-out. That traffic is intentionally not durable, and accepted best-effort submits or direct route attempts should not be persisted.

Remaining work: revisit this when relay or mailbox delivery is implemented. Persist only durable broadcast work and outcomes that are needed for relay-backed delivery, crash recovery, terminal bookkeeping, and any receipt semantics we explicitly define for durable delivery.

title: Shrink runtime working state to transaction-scoped group and row data
description: Refactor LocalRuntimeState into transaction-scoped helpers that operate over stable group metadata plus a small amount of row or dataset working state at a time. Prefer reading through the active store transaction and relying on read-your-own-writes semantics, rather than mirroring whole runtime datasets in component memory.
acceptance_criteria: The runtime no longer keeps long-lived full dataset images as its ground truth, row-scale helpers are sufficient for mutation paths, and the remaining in-memory state is clearly scoped to one active transaction.

title: Preserve listener notifications across partial inbound apply failures
description: During replication runtime inbound update draining, one causally-ready update batch may apply successfully before a later buffered batch fails. The current runtime only emits listener notifications after the whole drain completes, which can desynchronise the application-facing event stream from the already-committed local state. Track a follow-up to either emit incrementally with compensating behaviour or redesign the drain/apply contract so committed state and listener notifications cannot diverge.

title: Support Kompact-owned replication runtime topology embedding
description:
Reframed follow-up from the runtime-host topology refactor. The current DeliveryRuntimeHost topology is now structured enough for ordinary Rust async runtime setup, but it still owns the complete Kompact component topology externally. What is missing for a real Kompact-first API is an embedding surface that lets an existing Kompact component or topology create and own the replication runtime topology as child components under its own lifecycle, supervision, and configuration boundaries.

Design the required Kompact-facing APIs and ownership model: how callers supply or obtain a KompactSystem, how child topology components are registered, started, and stopped, how ports and actor refs are exposed without leaking internals, how failures propagate to the parent, and how this coexists with the current Rust async DeliveryRuntimeHost API.

Do not move the topology inward just for cosmetic ownership. The task is complete only when applications can set up replication through a Kompact-native integration path rather than only through the host wrapper.

title: Separate replication runtime payload encoding from encrypted byte transport
description: The replication runtime currently serialises runtime messages directly to bytes at the delivery-envelope boundary because payload handling is still byte-oriented for the future encryption setup. Track the follow-up to redesign this boundary around a proper payload abstraction (likely aligned with IoPayload or the eventual ciphertext envelope API) so in-memory runtime messages convert to wire format separately from byte or ciphertext serialisation.

title: Introduce a proper local delivery endpoint bind manager
description: The replication runtime currently binds its externally reachable UDP socket directly from DeliveryRuntimeHost startup via a temporary observer/queue path. Replace that with a dedicated internal component or service that owns the local endpoint binding lifecycle, starting with a simple single-preconfigured-port stub and leaving network-change handling for later. This should also eliminate the temporary RuntimeUdpObserver bind path from production runtime code.

title: Remove or consume runtime UDP observer backlog after startup bind
description: DeliveryRuntimeHost currently wires RuntimeUdpObserver to the shared IoBridge and queues every UdpIndication into an internal IndicationQueue. That queue is only consumed during external UDP socket startup binding, so ongoing socket-activity indications can accumulate unboundedly in long-running runtimes. Revisit this runtime-host design and either remove the observer after startup, scope it to bind confirmation only, or actively consume/drop irrelevant indications.

title: Persist local updates before returning PublishReceipt once durability exists
description: The first replication slice currently returns PublishReceipt once local in-memory state has been accepted and the outbound delivery submit has been issued. Once the replication runtime has a durable persistence layer, publish_changes should persist the local update before returning the receipt so the acknowledgement reflects durable local acceptance rather than only in-memory mutation.
close_reason: Completed; publish_changes commits row patches, the update record, and the group version vector before returning PublishReceipt.

title: Switch Kompact from vendored copy to upstream master
description: Stop patching crates.io to the vendored vendor/kompact-0.11.3 copy, point the workspace at https://github.com/kompics/kompact master instead, remove the vendored crate, refresh Cargo.lock, and fix any compile issues caused by the upstream git revision.
close_reason: Completed: workspace now uses upstream Kompact master and has been adapted to the upstream 0.12 API surface; follow-up flotsync-mfs tracks switching back to the published 0.12 release once available.

title: Remove vendored hierarchical_hash_wheel_timer and update to 1.4.0
description: Stop patching crates.io to the vendored hierarchical_hash_wheel_timer 1.3.0 copy, update the dependency path to the published 1.4.0 release, remove the vendored crate, and fix compile issues caused by the upstream API changes.

title: Add manual-time quiescence helper for timer-driven runtime tests
description: Proposal: keep the first implementation local to flotsync_udpour runtime tests and add a harness-level helper such as RuntimeHarness::advance_time_and_quiesce(duration). The helper should only be available on manual-timer harnesses. Step 1: call manual_timer.advance_by(duration). Step 2: wait until sender and receiver runtime components report that no timer-driven work remains due at the current logical time; add test-only idle/quiescent predicates on those components if the existing state probes are not sufficient. Step 3: round-trip one explicit test barrier through the probe side that observes externally visible outcomes, so any bridge frames or receiver deliveries triggered before the barrier are guaranteed to be enqueued before the helper returns. With that in place, rewrite the current candidate sites to use immediate buffered negative checks instead of short real-time silence windows: flotsync_udpour/src/runtime_tests.rs around shared_route_retransmissions_do_not_redeliver_before_tombstone_expiry, send_delay_and_window_pace_multipart_transmission, and no_longer_available_after_sender_retention_expiry. Do not generalise this into flotsync_io::test_support unless a second consumer appears; the problem is currently specific to UDPour's manual-time harness.
close_reason: Implemented manual-time due-timer harness and applied stable sites

title: Re-audit negative timing assertions after timer and wait-helper work
description: After controllable timer support and standardised eventually-style wait helpers land, revisit the remaining negative timing assertions across flotsync_io, flotsync_udpour, and flotsync_replication. Reclassify each case as either still semantically necessary, replaceable with a stronger causal assertion, or removable once the new test infrastructure is in place. Representative sites from the current analysis include UDPour runtime negative assertions, replication delivery full-stack silence checks, and raw/bridge IO no-event checks.

title: Support shared-coverage routes in direct GroupBroadcast fan-out
description: The minimal GroupBroadcast slice currently filters to exclusive direct routes and sends one copy per recipient. Extend it to group recipients by shared-capable route coverage and emit one transport submission per shared/broadcast-capable direct route where appropriate. Add tests that prove route grouping rather than one-send-per-recipient fan-out. Discovered while implementing flotsync-w6r.

title: Handle relay routes in GroupBroadcast semantic owner
description: The minimal GroupBroadcast slice currently ignores relay route discovery updates and relay-store confirmation traffic. Extend the semantic owner to track relay candidates and handle the relay delivery/control path, with explicit tests for the relay-specific behaviour. Discovered while implementing flotsync-w6r.

title: Revisit delivery ingress top-level payload clone for buffa view decode
description: The buffa migration preserved shallow delivery classification with MessageView, but decode_boundary_frame_if_interested currently has to materialise a contiguous byte clone via payload.create_byte_clone() before borrowed view traversal. Follow up by evaluating whether IoPayload can expose a zero-copy contiguous view for this path, whether buffa can decode views directly from the existing buffer shape, or whether a different boundary API would avoid the extra top-level copy without regressing the cheap classify-before-full-decode design.

title: Implement minimal GroupBroadcast semantic owner slice for end-to-end delivery tests
description: flotsync-d3g delivered the minimal direct ReliableDeliveryComponent path, but the analogous GroupBroadcast semantic owner slice is still missing. Add the smallest GroupBroadcast runtime/component path needed to consume the owner-specific inbound port from DeliveryIngress, emit the existing higher-level Deliver indication, and exercise one real end-to-end transport-to-semantic-owner path for group delivery.

title: Handle relay route discovery in ReliableDeliveryComponent
description: ReliableDeliveryComponent currently ignores relay-route discovery updates in the minimal direct-only slice. Follow up by consuming relay routes, integrating them into the reliable-delivery state machine, and supporting relay-based retries and mailbox flows.

title: Support multiple direct routes per reliable-delivery peer
description: ReliableDeliveryComponent currently keeps only one direct route per peer. Follow up by storing multiple discovery-provided direct routes per recipient, retrying on alternate routes after send failure, and updating route-selection policy accordingly.

title: Replace placeholder reliable-delivery signatures with real signing
description: ReliableDeliveryComponent currently uses placeholder_signed_footer() for direct envelope and recipient-ack messages in the minimal end-to-end slice. Replace this with the real signing/verification path once the concrete signature boundary is available, and remove the placeholder helper and TODO.

title: Implement minimal semantic ingress consumers for end-to-end delivery tests
description: Add the smallest GroupBroadcast and/or ReliableDelivery runtime slices needed to consume the new owner-specific inbound ports from DeliveryIngress and emit the existing higher-level Deliver indications, so the inbound path can be exercised end-to-end from RouteTransportManager through delivery ingress into a semantic owner. Scope this narrowly: no full queue/scheduler work, just enough wiring and tests to validate the new transport+ingress boundary in one integrated path.
notes:
2026-04-05 implementation plan:
1. Add a reusable cloneable one-shot completion helper (KClaimablePromise) around Arc<Mutex<Option<KPromise<...>>>> for Kompact-delivered indications that must remain Clone.
2. Implement ReliableDeliveryComponent as the first minimal semantic owner that supports the direct reliable-delivery path only.
3. Extend ReliableDeliveryDeliver to carry a cloneable processed-completion handle so receiver-side ack emission is gated on explicit processing rather than mere ingress receipt.
4. Add outbound reliable-envelope encoding and outbound recipient-ack encoding, plus inbound decoding from DeliveryIngress into domain objects.
5. Track sender-side work items through Pending to Observed recipient-ack state, and receiver-side pending inbound deliveries until processed completion triggers ack emission.
6. Build a reusable full-stack test harness per KompactSystem containing IoDriverComponent, IoBridge, RouteTransportManager, DeliveryIngressComponent, ReliableDeliveryComponent, the required wiring, discovery-route injection, external probes, and test-only state inspection.
7. Add a two-system end-to-end test using two full stacks with real UDPour/route-transport traffic in one direction for the envelope and the reverse direction for the ack; the test should complete the receiver-side processed handle immediately and assert sender-side ack observation.
2026-04-05 implementation progress:
- Added flotsync_utils::KClaimablePromise as a reusable cloneable one-shot completion helper over Arc<Mutex<Option<KPromise<...>>>> with focused unit tests.
- Implemented ReliableDeliveryComponent as the first minimal semantic owner for the direct reliable-delivery path.
- Extended ReliableDeliveryDeliver with a cloneable processed handle that gates recipient-ack emission on explicit processed completion rather than ingress receipt.
- Added direct reliable-envelope and recipient-ack protobuf boundary encoding/decoding plus sender-side and receiver-side in-memory tracking.
- Added a reusable two-system full-stack test harness covering IoDriverComponent, IoBridge, RouteTransportManager, DeliveryIngressComponent, ReliableDeliveryComponent, discovery injection, and external probes.
- Added an end-to-end test that sends a real direct reliable-delivery envelope through one full stack, completes the receiver-side processed handle, and observes the recipient ack return through the reverse stack.
- Verification: cargo fmt --all and cargo test -p flotsync_utils -p flotsync_replication --lib passed.
Refined the minimal direct reliable-delivery slice: switched direct route tracking to TrieMap, collapsed sender/inbound-ack retries onto one monotonic earliest-deadline timer queue, removed unused wall-clock timestamps from reliable sender state, moved reliable-delivery protobuf encode/decode into delivery/reliable_delivery/wire.rs, and added follow-up tasks flotsync-irn (multiple direct routes), flotsync-fx5 (relay route discovery), and flotsync-rp3 (lazy outbound serialisation).
Addressed follow-up review fixes: corrected the shared retry scheduler so stale-entry cleanup no longer drops overdue retries, renamed timer setup helpers from arm_* to set_*, rejected duplicate reliable submits by message_id, narrowed the member trie re-export to TrieMap/TrieSet, and added focused regression tests for overdue retry preservation and duplicate-submit retention of the original work item.
close_reason: Completed reliable-delivery semantic owner slice with end-to-end tests; group-broadcast split to follow-up task

title: Revisit mailbox fetch pagination design
description: Mailbox fetch pagination was removed from flotsync-zup to avoid baking in cursor semantics before the mailbox subsystem design is mature. Revisit whether mailbox fetches should be full fetches, monotonic sequence cursors, continuation tokens, or some other stateless paging model once mailbox storage, acknowledgement, and relay semantics are clearer.

title: Build UDPour performance measurement CLI
description: Create a CLI tool for measuring UDPour transfer performance with separate sender and receiver roles so both sides can be set up externally (for example over ssh). Scope the tool to: (1) measure unicast performance between one sender and one receiver; (2) measure multicast/broadcast performance between one sender and multiple receivers using the same sender/receiver setup model; (3) collect metrics that help localise bottlenecks or failure modes, including enough stage-specific information to tell whether problems came from send pacing/rate control, transport loss or repair traffic, receiver-side reassembly, scheduling, or completion latency; (4) provide a simple flotsync_tcp transfer baseline so we can compare against UDPour and identify limitations caused by poor rate control rather than the wider stack; and (5) provide a simple tokio-based TCP transfer baseline as an additional comparison point. The deliverable should make cross-run comparison straightforward and should report results in a form suitable for later analysis.

title: Implement TCP route transport backend
description: Implement the TCP side of RouteTransportManager, including session open/reuse, send handling, and mapping TCP session outcomes onto RouteTransportPort. This is intentionally split from the current UDP/datagram-transfer implementation work.

title: Make datagram-transfer receiver repair state incremental
description: Replace the current receiver repair path that rebuilds missing_parts from scratch and repeatedly re-splits roaring bitmaps on every poll. Track missing-part state incrementally and revisit the receiver-side storage structure so repair generation scales better for large multipart transfers.

title: Stream outbound multipart frames instead of materializing full frame vectors
description: Avoid building full Vec<PayloadFrame> batches for UDPour initial sends and NeedParts repair responses. Rework sender/runtime interaction so multipart payload frames can be emitted incrementally, reducing allocation and peak scheduler pressure for large logical messages and late repair traffic.
notes:
2026-04-04 implementation proposal:
1. Replace sender-side `SenderAction::SendPayloads { frames: Vec<PayloadFrame> }` with an owned payload-stream cursor that carries `message_id`, retained `IoPayload`, `checksum`, `part_count`, `max_part_payload_len`, retransmit flags, and an exact remaining-frame count.
2. Initial sends should iterate one contiguous part range; `NeedParts` repairs should own a `RoaringBitmap` into-iterator so requested parts are emitted incrementally in ascending order without materialising payload frames.
3. Carry the change through the UDPour runtime as well: extend the outbound queue from only concrete `QueuedDatagram`s to a queue entry enum that can hold either one concrete control datagram or one payload stream. When a payload stream reaches the queue head, materialise at most one payload datagram, dispatch it under the existing pacing/backpressure rules, and re-queue the stream at the front if more frames remain. This keeps payload ordering contiguous while still allowing newly queued control traffic to pre-empt.
4. Keep submit semantics unchanged. `OutboundTransfer.pending_initial_transmissions` should come from the stream's exact frame count instead of `frames.len()`, and `UDPourSubmitResult::Sent` should still mean that all initial payload datagrams were transport-acked.
5. Validation plan: update sender tests to consume the new cursor and assert part ordering plus retransmit flags; keep runtime coverage for paced multipart sends, repair retransmission, retention expiry to `NoLongerAvailable`, socket-close mid-send, and backpressure behaviour.
6. Rejected smaller variant: replacing the `Vec<PayloadFrame>` with an iterator while still enqueueing all datagrams up front would reduce sender allocation only partially and would not address the runtime queue pressure that motivated the task.

title: Integrate datagram transfer with discovery-published route candidates
description: Bridge flotsync_datagram_transfer from its current one-socket/SocketAddr runtime boundary to the broader route-transport architecture. This should define how discovery-published route candidates are consumed, how live route handles are managed, and how the datagram transfer runtime composes with the route-candidate world discussed for flotsync-187.

title: Queue outbound datagram-transfer frame sends instead of spawning per frame
description: Replace the current spawn_local-per-frame runtime send path in flotsync_datagram_transfer with a queued sender loop or equivalent owned dispatcher. The goal is to reduce scheduler overhead and provide one clearer outbound execution path for large multipart sends.
close_reason: Subsumed by flotsync-ml0 outbound dispatcher work

title: Control datagram transfer send rate and backpressure handling
description: Runtime currently reports logical send failure on most UDP Nacks and has no dedicated send-rate control/backpressure adaptation. Design and implement a bounded send-rate mechanism for flotsync_datagram_transfer so Backpressure can be handled without immediately surfacing a fatal logical send failure.

title: Decide inbound transport decode and demux ownership
description: Decide which component owns inbound wire parsing, signature verification boundaries, protobuf decoding, and demultiplexing from raw transport messages into GroupBroadcastPort and ReliableDeliveryPort Deliver indications. This was deferred during flotsync-187 and should be resolved before runtime implementation work gets too far.
notes:
2026-04-04 agreed design direction:
1. Rename the existing outbound-only `RouteTransportPort` to `RouteTransportOutboundPort` and add a sibling `RouteTransportInboundPort` for fully reassembled inbound logical payloads plus transport metadata.
2. Keep transport metadata transport-pure. UDP/datagram ingress must not expose a claimed authenticated sender on the route-transport port; verified sender identity belongs to the delivery-ingress layer after message-level parsing and signature verification.
3. Introduce a dedicated `DeliveryIngress` component between route transport and the semantic services. It owns shallow wire classification, full delivery-wire protobuf decode, message-level signature verification, and semantic demultiplexing.
4. Do not add a third application-bypass ingress port for now. `DeliveryIngress` should route only to semantic-owner-specific inbound ports: `GroupBroadcastInboundPort` and `ReliableDeliveryInboundPort`. The semantic services continue to emit the existing external `Deliver(...)` indications upward when appropriate.
5. The semantic-owner-specific inbound indications should carry shared metadata once plus one generated protobuf boundary frame, rather than repeating metadata in every enum variant.
6. Split the delivery wire at semantic boundaries with a small top-level discriminator: `DeliveryBoundaryFrame { group_broadcast | reliable_delivery }`. Inside each branch, the generated protobuf boundary frame owns the concrete variants.
7. Each concrete wire message must explicitly separate public routing/header fields from encrypted or bulky payloads using nested sub-messages such as `public_header` and `encrypted_payload`. We must not rely on protobuf field ordering alone to distinguish cheap routing data from encrypted content.
8. The earliest classification step in `DeliveryIngress` should parse only the top-level boundary plus the relevant `public_header`, derive a `DeliveryTargetHint`, and drop frames that are not locally interesting before full decode or signature verification.
9. The local-interest filter should use `active_groups` via `ArcSwap`, while `local_members` and `hosted_mailboxes` remain simpler shared `Arc<HashSet<...>>` values.
10. The semantic `MessageId` used by ingress is the delivery-domain message id from `flotsync_replication::delivery::shared`, not any transport-local id such as UDPour message ids.
11. `RecipientAckHeader` should gain `original_sender` so ingress can cheaply reject irrelevant acks, and the current generic relay-store confirmation shape should split into group and reliable variants carrying the sender/recipient scope needed for early routing.
12. Initial implementation slice: land the Rust-side port, metadata, and ingress scaffolding first, then follow with delivery-wire protobuf types/codecs and finally the concrete route-transport-to-ingress wiring.
2026-04-04 implementation progress:
- Renamed the route-transport semantic-delivery port to `RouteTransportOutboundPort` and added `RouteTransportInboundPort` plus `InboundTransportMeta` / `RouteTransportInboundDeliver`.
- Added `delivery::ingress` with `DeliveryIngress`, `DeliveryInterestConfig`, `DeliveryTargetHint`, `InboundDeliveryMeta`, and owner-specific inbound ports for group broadcast and reliable delivery.
- Extended `UDPourDeliver` with `socket_id` and wired `RouteTransportManager` to emit inbound transport deliveries for reassembled UDPour payloads using reconstructed UDP route metadata.
- Added the `original_sender` field to `RecipientAckHeader` and split relay-store confirmation into group and reliable variants.
- Current limitation: the new semantic inbound ports still carry temporary raw boundary payloads because delivery-wire protobuf boundary types and shallow parsing have not been implemented yet.
- Verification: `cargo test -p flotsync_udpour -p flotsync_replication` passed.
2026-04-04 implementation progress:
- Added generated messages/proto/delivery.proto with semantic-owner boundary frames and explicit public_header separation for envelope, mailbox, ack, and relay-store traffic.
- Added delivery::wire in flotsync_replication with a shallow protobuf classifier over IoPayload, early local-interest filtering, full protobuf decode only for relevant frames, and focussed unit tests.
- Updated delivery::ingress to use generated GroupBroadcastFrame / ReliableDeliveryFrame, route locally relevant frames onto the owner-specific inbound ports, and leave verified_sender unset pending a concrete signature verifier.
- Added ingress integration tests for active group frames and local recipient acks, and improved route-transport observability by warning on inbound UDPour deliveries from unknown socket ids.
- Verification: cargo fmt --all and cargo test -p flotsync_messages -p flotsync_replication -p flotsync_udpour passed.
2026-04-05 implementation adjustment:
- Removed mailbox pagination from the current delivery/mailbox wire and domain shapes. Mailbox fetch now assumes full fetch semantics, and mailbox batch no longer carries a cursor.
- Follow-up task flotsync-45z was created to revisit mailbox fetch pagination and cursor design once the mailbox subsystem semantics are clearer.
2026-04-05 wire clean-up:
- Flattened the delivery-wire signature footer so envelope-like messages now carry SignatureWire directly instead of a one-field SignatureFooterWire wrapper.
- Removed the unsupported custom signature-scheme variant from both the delivery wire and the shared delivery-domain SignatureScheme type. The wire now relies on protobuf enum unknown values for future unsupported schemes.
Reworked route-transport boundary towards actor-based outbound submission. Removed the outbound Kompact port and replaced it with RouteTransportActorMessage::Submit(Ask<...>) plus RouteTransportSubmitResult. The remaining RouteTransportPort is now indication-only with Request = Never explicitly documented as inbound-only. RouteTransportManager now fulfils pending submit promises directly, and manager tests now drive submits via actor ask instead of a probe port.
close_reason: Implemented and verified inbound transport decode/demux boundary

title: Evaluate buffa zero-copy view adoption opportunities
description: Assess where buffa MessageView / OwnedView types could materially improve flotsync without making the migration harder than necessary. Focus on hot decode paths in flotsync_messages and flotsync_discovery, identify where messages are read-only long enough to benefit from zero-copy borrowing, and spell out the API/lifetime changes required to use views safely across async or actor boundaries. Include a recommendation on whether to keep views disabled for the initial migration and introduce them in a second step, or adopt them immediately in a narrow set of paths.

comments.author: Lars Kroll
comments.text: Summary: the only current production use of buffa MessageView is the shallow delivery classifier. Discovery has no runtime decode path to optimise today, and most of flotsync_messages already starts from owned generated messages and immediately materialises owned domain values, so broader view adoption would add lifetime and API churn without much benefit. The only plausible next step is a narrow delivery-ingress experiment coupled to IoPayload contiguous access and possibly OwnedView, but that is better handled as the separate follow-up flotsync-r7p rather than widening view usage across the rest of the workspace.

title: Evaluate buffa use_bytes_type() migration and candidate fields
description: Assess whether buffa-build use_bytes_type() or selective use_bytes_type_in(...) would pay off in this workspace. Inventory the existing bytes-heavy fields and transport paths, identify where switching generated bytes fields from Vec<u8> to bytes::Bytes would reduce copies or simplify integration with IO/network code, and estimate the extra adaptation cost in codecs and public APIs. Include a recommendation on whether to keep the initial buffa migration on Vec<u8> compatibility first and follow with a targeted bytes::Bytes adoption step.

title: Track stable Rust compatibility
description: Plan and execute the work needed to make the workspace build and test on stable Rust. Cover removal or replacement of nightly-only features, toolchain/CI updates, and any crate-level API or dependency changes required for stable compatibility.

title: Revisit Kompact Recipient adapters for closure-carried tags
description: The current Kompact Recipient adapter surface only supports pure function forwarding, which makes it awkward to attach small runtime tags when routing TcpSessionEvent values. flotsync_io added its own direct tagged target wrapper around ActorRef as a local workaround. Investigate whether Kompact should offer a closure-carrying Recipient-like adapter or another first-class pattern for tagged forwarding so libraries do not need custom wrappers.

title: Add bounded framed-ingress collector utilities over IoPayload chunks
description: Add a small reusable framing utility for protocol handlers that need bounded accumulation and delimiter search across IoPayload chunk boundaries. The initial API should support collecting until a delimiter, reporting the trailing offset in the current chunk, and enforcing a byte limit without forcing each protocol implementation to rebuild the same buffered-ingress logic.

title: Add composite IoPayload chaining and payload traversal helpers
description: Extend IoPayload with a composite/chained variant so one logical send can interleave pooled leases, owned Bytes payloads, and static/literal fragments without forcing callers to flatten everything into one buffer first. Also add traversal/accessor APIs on IoPayload so client code does not need to match on the internal representation directly for common operations such as length, iteration, flattening, and cursor-style reads.

title: Add tagged TCP accept helper for Kompact recipients
description: Add convenience helpers around PendingTcpSession acceptance for common Kompact patterns, including direct accept-to-recipient and tagged accept helpers that inject a stable connection id or tag into the delivered session events. This should standardise the existing recipient-wrapping pattern for multiplexed servers without changing the underlying TcpSessionEvent shape.

title: Add TCP send-and-close helper for request/response handlers
description: Add a helper on the Kompact-facing TCP session API for the common pattern of sending one payload, waiting for transport acceptance/completion, and then closing the session gracefully. This should remove manual response_in_flight/response_sent bookkeeping from simple request/response components like the HTTP example.

title: Add higher-level TCP send helpers over the shared egress pool
description: Add convenience helpers on the Kompact-facing TCP session handle for common send paths such as send_bytes, send_static, and send_with(estimated_bytes, writer). These should use the bridge-owned egress pool internally so ordinary components do not have to reserve pooled capacity manually for routine sends.

title: Add lease ownership handoff between ingress and egress pools
description: Add an explicit API for moving uniquely-owned lease-backed payload memory from ingress to egress ownership so request/response protocols can reuse pool-backed bytes without re-copying them. The first cut should be restricted to whole-segment handoff for uniquely-owned leases and preserve the current backpressure semantics while the bytes remain in flight.

title: Extend flotsync_io HTTP example to keep-alive and stricter HTTP/1.1 semantics
description: Follow-up to flotsync-p8n. Extend the minimal flotsync_io HTTP server example from single-request/close behavior to sequential keep-alive handling on one TCP connection, stricter Host/header validation, and broader HTTP/1.1 protocol behavior (including representative 400/404/405/505 paths). Add a stronger protocol-oriented test harness, likely using Python h11, and optionally passive validation of captured exchanges.

title: Make Kompact test logging follow cargo test capture
description: In flotsync_io tests, replace Kompact's default terminal logger with a test-only logger that emits through the Rust test harness capture path so logs stay hidden in normal cargo test runs and appear under --no-capture/show-output.

title: Build flotsync_io netcat-style CLI example
description: Build a reference CLI application on top of flotsync_io/Kompact that can be launched in various TCP/UDP configurations to send and receive strings between instances. This should validate the session and shared-UDP model and serve as an example application for users of the new I/O stack.

title: Build flotsync_io simple HTTP server example
description: Build a minimal HTTP server example on top of flotsync_io/Kompact to validate the listener/session model with a familiar request/response workload and to provide a concrete example application for the new I/O stack.

title: Investigate TCP session cleanup after last session handle drops
description: The current Kompact TCP session adapter keeps session cleanup explicit and lifecycle-driven. Follow up on whether session components should be owned differently, or whether the adapter should support cleanup/GC when the last TcpSessionRef is dropped. This work was discovered during flotsync-hn4 review.

title: Refactor flotsync_io driver module layout
description: Split flotsync_io/src/driver.rs into smaller transport- and concern-specific modules before additional UDP/TCP work, keeping behavior unchanged. This was discovered while reviewing and extending flotsync-xko.
close_reason: Completed: split flotsync_io driver into focused registry, runtime, thread, tcp, and udp modules, moved UDP-specific tests beside the transport implementation, and kept behavior unchanged.

title: Investigate shareable ChunkLease cloning semantics for flotsync_io payloads
description: The current flotsync_io IoPayload clone path materialises ChunkLease into bytes::Bytes because Kompact Port traffic must be Clone while ChunkLease is uniquely owned. Evaluate whether we can preserve lease-backed payload sharing across clones, or otherwise reduce the memory cost of cloning lease-backed payloads. Context: freeform I/O API skeleton in flotsync_io.

title: Add soak and lightweight performance instrumentation
description: Add counters/tracing and long-running stress tests to validate correctness and resource behavior before optimization/sharding work.
close_reason: Deferred: not doing soak/perf work on this branch

title: Audit HashMap/HashSet hasher usage and performance tradeoffs
description: Audit all HashMap/HashSet usage across the workspace and decide where cryptographic-strength hashing is unnecessary. For each call site, classify risk profile (trusted/untrusted keys), throughput sensitivity, and collision-abuse exposure; then choose and document the appropriate hasher strategy (keep default SipHash where needed, use faster non-crypto hashers where safe). Include a rollout plan, migration checklist, and benchmark guidance for high-traffic paths.

title: Specify Replication sub-protocol state machine and flows
description:
Future replication protocol specification outside the manual replication slice.

Current status: the narrow live path needed by the manual slice is already implemented. The runtime can publish and receive UpdateBatch messages for fixed-membership live peers, uses read_versions on the wire, deduplicates by UpdateId, and drops unknown-group live updates instead of buffering pre-bootstrap races.

Remaining work: define the full replication sub-protocol for offline and long-running operation, including Summary, NeedRange, Snapshot, Ack, reconnect and resynchronisation flows, causality-blocked replay policy, migration, and group close. This is not required before the current checklist slice works.

title: Specify GroupBroadcast queue model and delivery semantics
description:
Future GroupBroadcast durable-delivery work outside the manual replication slice.

Current status: the queue model and delivery semantics have been specified in docs/group_broadcast_queue_model.md. The important slice boundary is that BestEffort work is non-durable and is dropped on restart. The manual replication slice only uses best-effort direct GroupBroadcast for live UpdateBatch traffic.

Remaining work: apply the queue model when relay or mailbox delivery is implemented. That future work needs durable active work, relay store acknowledgement state, crash recovery, terminal outcomes, and any explicit delivery receipt semantics for durable routes. It does not block flotsync-5j0.

title: Define Application/Dataset namespacing in replication API
description: Specify how Application and Dataset are represented as public API namespacing for rows while keeping the internal replication model group/schema-centric. Document naming, identifiers, and mapping rules from namespaced API calls to internal group/schema operations.

title: Design catch-up fulfillment policy v1 (UpdateBatch-first)
description: Define catch-up behavior for NeedRange in two phases: v1 must always fulfill via UpdateBatch, and v2 adds Snapshot + trailing UpdateBatch optimization. Specify selection rules, correlation handling, and how this composes with GroupBroadcast delivery classes.
design:
Agreed implementation plan for catch-up fulfilment v1.

CNS-001. Scope v1 to manual/live fixed-membership peers, durable local stores, and existing direct group broadcast delivery. Do not include Snapshot fulfilment, relay/mailbox repair, automatic discovery, public manual catch-up API, or encrypted/authenticated envelope changes.

CNS-002. Extend `ReplicationUpdateFilter` with a simple single-producer linear range query, for example producer member index plus inclusive start/end versions and a bounded limit. The storage layer must not compare full read-version vectors or decide causal readiness.

CNS-003. Add `NeedRange` as a repair signal, not an RPC. It carries `group_id` and one or more single-producer inclusive ranges. It does not need a correlation id; completion is determined by local durable version-vector progress.

CNS-004. Add catch-up `UpdateBatch` as the fulfilment message: a bounded list of existing `Update` messages. Responses may partially satisfy a requested range. Empty fulfilment is not useful on the wire for v1.

CNS-005. Use `GroupBroadcast` for `NeedRange` and catch-up `UpdateBatch` in v1. Do not add more per-peer lifecycle state to `ReliableDelivery`; duplicate responses are acceptable and must be harmless.

CNS-006. Introduce `CatchUpManager` as the owner of repair protocol mechanics: outbound needed-range state, narrowing/removal when local progress advances, retry/backoff, broadcasting `NeedRange`, receiving inbound `NeedRange`, loading matching stored updates, and broadcasting catch-up `UpdateBatch`.

CNS-007. Keep `ReplicationRuntimeComponent` responsible for diagnosis. It observes summaries, inbound updates, and stuck pending apply state; computes concrete missing single-producer ranges; and sends those ranges to `CatchUpManager` as local `NeedVersions` messages.

CNS-008. `CatchUpManager` may talk to the store only to fulfil other nodes' `NeedRange` requests. It should not inspect pending apply state or compare read versions to decide what this node is missing.

CNS-009. `SummaryRequest` still returns the requested `Summary`. When a received summary reports versions ahead of local state, the replication component computes missing producer ranges and notifies `CatchUpManager`.

CNS-010. Inbound `Update` handling should trigger catch-up when it sees producer gaps from `update_id.version > expected_next`, dependency gaps from `read_versions > local_versions`, or pending updates that remain blocked after the apply attempt.

CNS-011. Pending apply handling should report why it is stuck by deriving missing ranges from current local VV and the pending updates' producer/read versions, then notifying `CatchUpManager`.

CNS-012. When receiving a `NeedRange`, `CatchUpManager` validates that the sender is a member of the target group, loads applied/persisted updates for each requested single-producer range, and broadcasts a bounded `UpdateBatch`.

CNS-013. For catch-up `UpdateBatch`, validate only that the batch sender is some member of the replication group it is sending updates for. Do not require the sender to be the original producer of each contained update.

CNS-014. For each contained `Update`, derive the logical producer from `update_id.node_index` using the group's canonical member order, then feed that producer into the existing persist/apply path. Do not add redundant catch-up-layer validation against the same producer information.

CNS-015. Reuse existing durable persist/apply logic for every update delivered by catch-up. Duplicate `UpdateBatch` delivery, overlapping ranges, and repeated broadcasts must be harmless through existing persisted-update equality checks and already-applied detection.

CNS-016. Retry policy v1: after a short interval, unresolved ranges are rebroadcast, narrowed first from current local progress. There is no per-peer response tracking or explicit ack.

CNS-017. Scenario expectations should change after implementation: two peers with two independent changes should converge after two syncs instead of three, and a restarted/down replica should catch up after seeing a summary or update frontier.

CNS-018. Acceptance tests should cover the store range filter, summary-triggered need, update-triggered need, pending-apply-triggered need, partial `UpdateBatch`, duplicate `UpdateBatch`, third-party fulfilment, and range narrowing after progress.
close_reason: Implemented catch-up fulfilment v1 and added runtime coverage; checklist three-sync issue remains an app-level manual-sync semantics limitation.

title: Define common outer protocol envelope
description:
Future common-envelope specification outside the manual replication slice.

Current status: the manual slice can use the existing delivery envelopes and runtime payload framing. No common outer envelope redesign is required before best-effort live UpdateBatch replication and the checklist example are functional.

Remaining work: specify the shared outer envelope across PeerDiscovery/Tracking, GroupBroadcast, and Replication. This should settle message ids, sender ids, optional group ids, payload kind, correlation ids, extension strategy, and which header fields remain cleartext once group-scoped payload encryption is introduced. This should line up with the later security and payload-redesign work, not block flotsync-5j0.

title: Define replication-group domain model and lifecycle
description:
Future replication-group domain model specification outside the manual replication slice.

Current status: the manual slice already has the fixed-membership model it needs: GroupId, canonical ordered GroupMembers, local member index, VersionVector, and durable ReplicationGroupRecord storage for the configured static group.

Remaining work: define the richer long-term group domain model for schema epochs, key-material references, relay-set references, membership changes, and lifecycle states such as Active, CatchUp, CausalityBlocked, Migrating, and Closed. These concepts are not required before the static-group checklist slice works.

title: Design database-level APIs and replication group model
description: Define next-layer APIs above schema-level operations, including replication group concepts and boundaries. Evaluate crate placement (flotsync_messages vs flotsync_core) and specify how database-level operations compose with schema-level operations.
close_reason: Completed on codex/flotsync-pzi: finalized flotsync_replication API sketch updates (module split, boxed-future utility reuse, batched provider contracts, IDs/errors submodules, and one-shot invitation responder).

title: Add ordered finite-state register builder
description: Replace the raw null_index-based finite_state_register helper with an ordered-values builder, add OrderedValue plus importable NULL, add validation and Display for NullablePrimitiveValueArray, and migrate existing helper call sites/tests.
close_reason: Completed ordered finite-state register builder, OrderedValue/NULL convenience API, NullablePrimitiveValueArray Display, and migration of public-facing helper call sites/tests.

title: Refine deleted-row error and test ergonomics
description: Add a dedicated ModifyDeletedRow operation error, revert only inline origin chunk fields in proto metadata to optional while keeping HistoryId chunk_index required, fix the LatestValueWins chunk_index comment, replace remaining assert!(matches!(...)) with assert_matches!, and simplify selected tests to use the new schema/value helpers.
close_reason: Completed deleted-row error refinement, proto origin chunk-index fix, LatestValueWins chunk-index comment correction, assert_matches cleanup, and test ergonomics updates.

title: Adjust tombstone row semantics and indexed-id cleanup
description: Update InMemoryData row semantics so tombstones still count in len/is_empty and are included in snapshots, return tombstoned rows from get_row, allow applying remote updates to deleted rows while still rejecting local modify_row on deleted rows, remove redundant total-order helper constructors, move uuid to workspace deps, clean up flotsync_messages indexed-id aliases/imports, and make proto chunk_index fields required.
close_reason: Completed tombstone row semantics update, indexed-id/proto cleanup, workspace uuid dependency move, and related test/protobuf adjustments.

title: Add proper LinearList diff helper
description: modify_row currently computes LinearList updates by dropping no-ops and otherwise emitting a simple full-replacement delete+insert sequence. Add a dedicated list diff helper, similar in spirit to text::linear_diff, to produce smaller list CRDT update batches.
close_reason: Completed public LinearList diff helper using similar slice diff, wired modify_row to use minimal list operations, added list/public API tests, and validated with cargo test -p flotsync_data_types.

title: Add schema-level default field values
description: Follow up on table insert ergonomics. Allow schema fields to declare default values so insert_row does not require every field to be provided explicitly when a schema-defined default can be materialized safely.

title: Implement row decode APIs for InMemoryDataRow
description: Implement RowOperations for InMemoryDataRow and Decode for Rust primitives, owned collections/slices, String, NaiveDate, and UnixTimestamp against InMemoryFieldValue. Include nullable decode support and typed error reporting for mismatches.

title: Benchmark node-oriented vs experimental columnar HistorySnapshot formats and record a recommendation
description: Add a benchmark harness comparing the current node-oriented HistorySnapshot format with the experimental columnar alternative for encoded size, encode/decode cost, and reconstruction cost, then record the results and recommendation.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Benchmarks compare current and experimental HistorySnapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative fixtures. 2) The benchmark harness includes medium and large histories plus tombstone-heavy cases. 3) Results are written down with a clear keep/discard/iterate recommendation.

title: Add correctness fixtures and roundtrip tests for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot
description: Add representative history fixtures and correctness coverage for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot codec, including boundary nodes, tombstones, null payloads, and empty arrays where allowed by schema.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Deterministic fixtures cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList history streams with representative edge cases. 2) Experimental-format roundtrip tests verify node equivalence and reconstructed CRDT integrity. 3) Edge cases cover boundary nodes, tombstones, nullable values, and empty array payloads where valid.

title: Prototype the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot wire format and codec
description: Add the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot protobuf messages and a low-level benchmark-only codec path that stores node metadata separately from dense value arrays.
acceptance_criteria: 1) datamodel.proto contains an experimental columnar HistorySnapshot representation with node metadata and a dense PrimitiveArrayValue payload section. 2) flotsync_messages has low-level encode/decode helpers for the experimental format covering LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList. 3) The experimental codec remains isolated from the current public snapshot adapter path.

title: Implement direct protobuf conversions for schema operations
description: Implement direct protobuf message conversions for SchemaOperation<UpdateId> and OperationValue<UpdateId> across the full datamodel operation surface, including history-backed and state-based field variants.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Protobuf conversions cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, LinearList, MonotonicCounter, TotalOrderRegister, and TotalOrderFiniteStateRegister operation variants. 2) LinearString and LinearList insert and delete forms are both represented. 3) The helpers validate or preserve enough structure to roundtrip back into the datamodel operation APIs.

title: Implement protobuf snapshot payload codecs for the datamodel surface
description: Implement specialized protobuf encode and decode helpers for all schema snapshot value shapes, including state values, NullableBasicValue, PrimitiveValueArray, SnapshotNode payloads, IdWithIndex<UpdateId>, tombstones, and boundary nodes.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Snapshot payload codecs cover every value shape needed by SchemaSnapshotEncoder and SchemaSnapshotDecoder. 2) Primitive-specialized array encodings are implemented for efficient wire layout. 3) History node codecs preserve stable link, tombstone, and boundary-node semantics.

title: Implement protobuf snapshot visitors and dataset decoders
description: Implement SchemaSnapshotEncoder, SchemaSnapshotDecoder, DataSnapshotEncoder, and DataSnapshotDecoder in flotsync_messages for the specialized protobuf format, following the existing schema/datamodel snapshot flow end to end.
acceptance_criteria: 1) flotsync_messages provides working protobuf-backed implementations of the schema snapshot visitor and decoder traits. 2) Dataset-level row encoding and lazy row decoding work through InMemoryData encode_data_snapshots and decode_data_snapshots. 3) The implementation uses the specialized snapshot payload codecs rather than per-CRDT bespoke paths.

title: Add protobuf schema and UpdateId wire access for datamodel transport
description: Define protobuf messages for the schema snapshot and schema operation surface specialized to UpdateId, and add the minimal flotsync_core API needed for flotsync_messages to construct and inspect UpdateId during wire conversion.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Protobuf messages cover dataset snapshots, row snapshots, history node streams, state field payloads, and schema operations using UpdateId. 2) flotsync_core exposes the minimal public API needed to encode and decode UpdateId outside the crate. 3) The message layout is documented well enough for the codec tasks to implement against it.

title: Add protobuf roundtrip coverage and compatibility docs
description: Add end-to-end tests and documentation for the specialized protobuf transport, covering dataset snapshot roundtrips, exhaustive schema operation coverage, and compatibility details for field mappings, UpdateId encoding, tombstones, and boundary nodes.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Tests roundtrip dataset snapshots through the protobuf snapshot visitor and decoder implementations. 2) Tests cover the exhaustive schema operation surface using the existing datamodel test support. 3) Documentation describes compatibility expectations and the stable mapping for UpdateId, tombstones, and boundary nodes.

title: Add unit tests for schema/datamodel/operations.rs
description: Add reusable unit tests for operation serialization/deserialization and schema-level partial operation flow, mirroring snapshot test strategy.

title: Review operations datamodel shape and field-level API
description: After snapshot review approval, review schema/datamodel operations API shape and confirm it is final enough to lock serialization and tests.

title: Review snapshot datamodel + InMemoryData serialization flow
description: Review the new schema/datamodel snapshot shape and InMemoryData::visit_row_snapshot integration, including ID-shape split (LVW uses base Id; linear history uses IdWithIndex<Id>) and test coverage.

title: Implement protobuf serialization for flotsync_data_types in flotsync_messages
description: Define and implement protobuf encoding/decoding for flotsync_data_types snapshots and operations in flotsync_messages, covering LinearString, LinearList, and LinearLatestValueWins. Include primitive-specialized encoding paths where applicable and stable mappings for IDs and tombstones.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Protobuf schemas/messages added for snapshot and operation payloads for LinearString, LinearList, and LinearLatestValueWins. 2) flotsync_messages exposes conversion helpers between protobuf payloads and flotsync_data_types snapshot/operation APIs. 3) Roundtrip tests verify encode/decode correctness for all three types, including deleted/tombstone nodes and boundary nodes. 4) Documentation describes field mappings and compatibility expectations.

title: Reduce HPKE encapsulated-key conversion churn
description: Follow-up from flotsync_security review. The public HpkeCiphertext type now stores the fixed-width DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) encapsulated key as a 32-byte array and exposes typed from_parts/into_parts helpers. Remaining work, if this still proves worth doing, is to centralise the upstream hpke Serializable/Deserializable conversion helpers and profile whether avoiding the HPKE ciphertext Vec returned by the library is worth a larger API change.

title: Precompute canonical group crypto context if hot
description: Follow-up from flotsync_security review. group_nonce and group_aad now encode sender identity directly from Identifier segments rather than allocating sender.to_string(), but both paths still rebuild the canonical context bytes on each seal/open. If group traffic becomes hot, introduce a small precomputed context type so nonce derivation, AAD construction, and signature transcripts can reuse canonical frame/member context bytes without repeated segment iteration or temporary encoding.

title: Evaluate certificate or PKI-based trust for flotsync_security
description: Follow-up after the flotsync_security MVP: decide whether Flotsync should support certificates, PKI, or another structured trust model beyond copied public JWKS files and discovery-published keys. This should compare operational complexity, mobile or desktop UX, local-first expectations, revocation, and compatibility with existing MemberIdentity values.
acceptance_criteria: There is a written decision or implementation plan for whether certificate or PKI support belongs in Flotsync, how it maps to MemberIdentity, how revocation would work, and what remains intentionally unsupported.

title: Evaluate parallel runtime topology startup
description: Runtime host startup currently starts topology components sequentially even after moving to Kompact async lifecycle APIs. Investigate which topology components can safely start concurrently, document dependency ordering, and update startup to parallelise only independent components.

title: Switch Kompact dependency back to a published 0.12 release
description: Follow-up after flotsync-dya. The workspace currently points at Kompact master because the needed 0.12 APIs were only available upstream. Once an actual Kompact 0.12 release is published, replace the git patch/dependency with the crates.io release, refresh Cargo.lock, and verify the workspace still builds against the released API.
close_reason: Switched workspace back to the published Kompact 0.12.0 crates.io release and verified the workspace.

title: Introduce runtime message proto conversion trait
description: Runtime message encoding/decoding currently uses ad hoc to_proto/from_proto helpers for Update, Summary, and related wire forms. Extract a small trait or consistent conversion pattern in a cleanup slice.

title: Upstream Kompact config array iteration API
description: Kompact ConfigLookup currently lets consumers select an indexed element, but does not expose selected array values as a slice or iterator. flotsync_replication's static peer route parser currently has to probe get_index(index) until MissingIndex. Upstream an API such as ConfigLookup::as_array()/iter_array() or a typed iterator so consumers can avoid guessing indices, then update flotsync_replication/src/runtime/host/discovery.rs to use it.
close_reason: Kompact config array iteration API is upstreamed and flotsync use-sites now use array_entries()

title: Decide how delivery absolute-time APIs should be represented and tested
description: The delivery domain still exposes uncovered absolute wall-clock types in flotsync_replication/src/delivery/shared.rs and flotsync_replication/src/delivery/contracts.rs (SystemTime in retry_after, observed_at, and first_submitted_at). These are not used in the current flow, but they are outside the new Kompact/manual-timer coverage and need an explicit design decision: either keep wall-clock semantics behind a dedicated injected clock, or move these APIs onto a different representation that matches the timer-controlled runtime model. If we keep absolute timestamps, evaluate whether the hierarchical_hash_wheel_timer simulation timer or a similar virtual wall-clock seam should be replicated so tests can control them as well. Discovered while implementing flotsync-jgg.

title: Decouple semantic delivery tests from route transport and IO with scripted doubles
description: Introduce scripted route-discovery and route-transport test doubles so GroupBroadcast and ReliableDelivery semantics can be tested without full flotsync_io and UDPour bring-up. Keep a smaller set of full-stack smoke tests for end-to-end signal; the goal here is better debuggability and isolation for semantic-owner tests, not to replace the smoke coverage.
acceptance_criteria: GroupBroadcast and ReliableDelivery each have semantic-level tests that exercise routing outcomes, delivery, acknowledgements, and failure handling without binding real sockets or starting the full transport stack; a smaller curated set of end-to-end smoke tests remains for transport integration coverage.

title: Improve reliable-delivery outbound serialisation laziness
description: ReliableDeliveryComponent currently wraps pre-encoded protobuf frames in FlotsyncSerializable, which keeps an intermediate buffer around instead of serialising lazily into transport-owned buffers. Follow up by revisiting the boundary once the protobuf implementation is upgraded and remove the unnecessary intermediate representation where practical.

title: Upstream LocalActor helper to Kompact
description: Contribute the LocalActor blanket Actor implementation helper upstream to Kompact so the workspace can eventually drop the local patch. Include API discussion, trait bounds, and migration notes from the flotsync local usage.
close_reason: No longer needed after moving LocalActor helper back into flotsync_utils and dropping the Kompact patch

title: Add Display impls across schema type hierarchy
description: Follow up on schema ergonomics. Add readable fmt::Display implementations across Schema, Field, ReplicatedDataType, and related schema/value types so schema definitions and errors can be rendered for humans without relying on Debug.
close_reason: Implemented SQL-like Display formatting across schema types with deterministic ordering, TBLPROPERTIES, backtick identifiers, ARRAY<T> types, and single-quoted state literals; added unit coverage.

title: Design row tombstone garbage collection
description: We now retain tombstoned rows in InMemoryData and include them in snapshots to preserve ordering and convergence semantics. Follow up with a design for when and how tombstoned rows and their ids can be garbage-collected safely without violating replication correctness.

title: Adapt decode implementations to ?Sized Decode signature
description: Update the in-memory decode module to match the relaxed ?Sized Decode trait shape, including any Cow / boxed-slice adjustments required by the new signature.

title: Benchmark a columnar HistorySnapshot format against the current node-oriented encoding
description: Once the baseline protobuf snapshot encoder/decoder exists, add an alternative HistorySnapshot storage format that stores node metadata columnar-style and packs node payloads into shared value arrays recoverable from the metadata. Benchmark the baseline and alternative formats against each other for encoded size plus encode/decode performance on representative history snapshots, and capture the tradeoffs and recommendation.
acceptance_criteria: 1) An alternative HistorySnapshot representation is prototyped for benchmarking, using columnar node metadata plus shared value storage. 2) Benchmarks compare the baseline and alternative snapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative datasets. 3) Results are recorded with a clear comparison and recommendation on whether to keep, discard, or further iterate on the columnar format.

title: Add InMemoryData helper API to create row operations
description: Add reusable in-memory orchestration for constructing full-row operations (partial field updates) so tests and transports do not hand-assemble per-field operations.
close_reason: Completed TableOperations public API, in-memory row operation helpers, protobuf row-operation wiring, UUID row ids, indexed LVW ids, tombstoned deletes, ergonomic field builders/macros, and public API tests.
